title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 2 - 2017: Fixed Livy

enrichment.summary: Kyle describes working through the night on a Roman history timeline website, adding 7,500 date links and YouTube playlist icons. He plans to spend the next week or two attaching public-domain Latin historical texts (Livy, Tacitus's Annals and Histories, Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire) to the timeline, sourcing them from WikiSource and Project Gutenberg.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

stances.position: Finds it beautiful how much Roman history was recorded across so many detailed books
Focused on attaching source texts to timeline before worrying about next steps; content to work and smoke rather than exercise

entities: Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log for May 2nd, 2017. I didn't do mine yesterday.
For May 1st, I was too hooked on my site.
Today, I decided to do a new thing.
Yesterday, last time I talked, I was doing the time line, Roman timeline.
And then I went into Roman time chart to make the links for all the different dates so that I could attach them to the timeline.
And a link to that date from my blog and page and from the books and stuff.
And so that took a while because it was like 7,500 links I had to attach to it.
And then I also attached the little YouTube icons because it will be the playlist for my video page too.
So that took a long time and I was working until like 6 o'clock in the morning this morning on that.
But I finished it like I wanted to.
And then I got up kind of late, later today, but not that late.
You know, I was like, actually, yeah, I was got up at like 11 or something.
And yeah, I was going.
And so working all day.
I decided today, though, instead of going and working on the timeline itself, you can condense the words, to do the audio books.
I mean, the books for the timeline.
And I have Livy and I found out because I went on WikiSource.
I got this one originally from like Gutenberg or something.
But I went to WikiSource and I saw that there's more to Livy.
All the lost books they had, like summaries from people who knew the book or something.
So I'm attaching all that and then summaries for books 11 through 20.
And I won't be done today.
But when I'm done with Livy, then I'll attach it to the timeline on the vertical part.
Or you click it and it'll take you to the book.
And maybe on the, yeah, horizontal part, maybe on the vertical part, too.
But, yeah, I have Livy.
And then after Livy, I'm going to put Tacitus, Annals, Histories, all of Julius Caesar, and then all of Decline and Fall of Roman Empire on my website.
And then attach it to the timeline to link it.
Because all those books, it's a beautiful how Rome has so much history recorded.
So Livy's like 3,500 pages over like 700 years from like seven, or more than that, for like 1,000 years.
But then you have Annals, and I think that's a huge book, Annals and Histories from Tacitus.
It covers 69 to 98.
It's only like 29 years with just all these books.
So that's cool.
A lot of detail.
And then Decline and Fall is like 8,800 pages.
So all about Rome.
So, yeah, I feel like when this is done, those books are on.
And attach to the timeline, I'll be like another step closer to being ready to do my show.
So, and with all the Decline and Fall book, hell, I could take it going until I'm like 90 or something.
So, but, yeah, so that's what I'm going to do for the next week or two.
Just attach those books.
And then I'll be fired up to attach the, do the Google Earth, finish the Google Earth stuff.
And then I'll be like ready.
It'll be awesome.
So, yeah, I'm going to start smoking again.
Yeah, keep smoking wee for, and not exercising, just working on the side.
Yeah, but it's exciting.
That's enough, that's enough goals for now, though.
I don't need to start thinking about what I'll do after that.
So, ooh, I think we've talked long enough.
Okay, peace out.
Peace out.
